mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a powerful backup and restore operator for Kubernetes, allowing users to back up cluster resources and persistent volumes, migrate applications, and replicate environments. It is particularly useful for disaster recovery and migration scenarios.

steps.title: Install Velero CLI
steps.description:
Download and install the Velero CLI for version v1.15. Use the following commands to install it on your local machine:

```bash
# Download the Velero CLI
curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.15.0/velero-v1.15.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz -o velero.tar.gz
# Extract the tarball
 tar -xvf velero.tar.gz
# Move the binary to your PATH
sudo mv velero-v1.15.0-linux-amd64/velero /usr/local/bin/
```
Verify the installation:
```bash
velero version
```

steps.title: Install Velero on Kubernetes
steps.description:
Use the following command to install Velero on your Kubernetes cluster. Ensure you have a compatible cloud provider configured (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure):

```bash
velero install --provider aws --bucket <YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --secret-file <YOUR_AWS_SECRET_FILE> --use-volume-snapshots=true --backup-location-config region=<YOUR_REGION>
```
Replace `<YOUR_BUCKET_NAME>`, `<YOUR_AWS_SECRET_FILE>`, and `<YOUR_REGION>` with your specific configurations.

steps.title: Verify Velero Installation
steps.description:
Check if Velero is running correctly by executing:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n velero
```
You should see the Velero server pod in a Running state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure Velero for automatic backups by creating a schedule:

```bash
velero schedule create daily-backup --schedule="0 1 * * *" --ttl=720h0m0s
```
This command creates a daily backup at 1 AM.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
To uninstall Velero, run the following command:

```bash
velero uninstall
```
This will remove all Velero resources from your cluster. Additionally, manually delete any cloud resources (like S3 buckets) associated with Velero if necessary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
To upgrade Velero from v1.14 to v1.15, first back up your existing Velero configuration:

```bash
velero backup create pre-upgrade-backup --include-cluster-resources
```
Then, install the new version:
```bash
velero install --provider aws --bucket <YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --secret-file <YOUR_AWS_SECRET_FILE> --use-volume-snapshots=true --backup-location-config region=<YOUR_REGION>
```
Finally, verify the upgrade:
```bash
velero version
```
If issues arise, you can rollback by restoring from the backup created earlier.

troubleshooting.title: Velero pod not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Velero pod is not starting, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs deployment/velero -n velero
```
Common issues include incorrect cloud provider configuration or missing permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Backup fails with an error
troubleshooting.description:
If a backup fails, check the backup logs:
```bash
velero backup describe <BACKUP_NAME>
```
Ensure that the bucket is accessible and that you have the correct IAM permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Restoration issues
troubleshooting.description:
If restoration fails, verify the backup status:
```bash
velero backup describe <BACKUP_NAME>
```
Ensure that the backup is completed and not in a failed state.

versionNotes.changes: This version includes improved error handling and additional backup options.
versionNotes.deprecations: The `--use-volume-snapshots` flag is now required for volume backups.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for additional cloud providers and enhanced CLI commands.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of Velero will have the Velero server running in your Kubernetes cluster, with the CLI configured on your local machine. You should be able to create backups and restore them as needed.

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.15.0/velero-v1.15.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz -o velero.tar.gz

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and access to a cloud provider for backup storage.
